heading: A narrative of the Lord's wonderful dealings with - Afro-Americana Imprints From LCP, no. S1374 - 1785

record.Title: A narrative of the Lord's wonderful dealings with John Marrant, a black (now going to preach the Gospel in Nova-Scotia) born in New-York, in North-America. Taken down from his own relation, arranged, corrected, and published by the Rev. Mr. Aldridge.
record.Author: Marrant, John, 1755-1791
record.Subjects: Marrant, John, 1755-1791; Indian captivities; Afro-Americans--Autobiography; Afro-American writers; Converts--Autobiography; Afro-Americana
record.Publication Source: Afro-Americana Imprints From LCP, no. S1374
record.Publication Information: London printed and sold by Gilbert and Plummer, 1785. And sold at the Chapel in Jewry-Street; and by all booksellers and newscarriers in town and country, [1785]
record.Notes: Signatures: [A]♯ B-E♯. Lib. Company. Afro-Americana, 2nd ed. Suppl. 1374; ESTC T81815; Howes, W. U.S.iana (2nd ed.), A111; Ayer Coll. 7
record.Edition: The fourth edition, with additions and notes explanatory.

A_narrative_of_the_Lords_wonderful_deali.description: A narrative of the Lord's wonderful dealings with John Marrant, a black (now going to preach the Gospel in Nova-Scotia) born in New-York, in North-America Taken down from his own relation, arranged, corrected, and published by the Rev. Mr. Aldridge.
